about:
Adeola Alain Patrick AINA is a computing and technologies enthusiast, and a seasoned network engineer with degrees in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science.
He began his entrepreneurial journey in 1999 by founding one of the first IT service and engineering companies in the Togolese Republic—Technologies Réseaux & Solutions (TRS), which provides services and solutions in network architecture, information systems security, and capacity building.
Under his leadership, TRS has supported numerous companies and initiatives across the African continent in adopting new technologies. Building on this success, he founded Digital Intelligence Services, a company focused on digital transformation and evolved solutions from TRS offerings.
Mr. AINA is a member of the international technical team of the Network Startup Resource Center (NSRC) and serves as a DNSSEC Roadshow consultant for ICANN in the region.
As Chief Technology Officer, he played a key role in establishing the first regional Education and Research Network for West and Central Africa. He also served the African Internet Registry (AFRINIC) for 11 years in multiple roles, including Acting CEO.
A retired AFNOG instructor, he now serves as co-convener of the initiative. He contributed to ICANN’s Security and Stability Advisory Committee for several years and was a member of the Security and Stability Review Team 2 (SSR2).
He has also served as a trusted community representative and “Crypto Officer” for DNSSEC at the root and was a member of the Internet Society Board of Trustees.
His unwavering commitment has earned him several recognitions, including the “Nii AWARD 2012” and the prestigious “Jonathan B. Postel Award” in 2019, among others from the global technical and scientific community.
name: Mr. Adeola Alain Patrick
desc: Adeola Alain Patrick AINA is a network engineer and tech entrepreneur known for advancing digital transformation across Africa and his global Internet contributions.

name: Mr. Kwesi Amoafo Yeboah
about:
Mr. Kwesi Amoafo-Yeboah received a BS degree in Engineering and Petro-Physics from San Francisco State University, San Francisco, California - USA.

Subsequently, he has held electronic design engineer positions at Fisher-Berkeley Corporation where he focused on the design and testing of hospital communication systems. He moved from there to hold various engineering and management positions with oil service giant, Schlumberger Technology Corporation.

From 1986 to present he has founded and managed several companies in the fields of financial services, telecommunications and equities management. He was responsible for the successful introduction of the Western Union Money Transfer Service to Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, Uganda, Ethiopia, Eritrea and Zambia.

He is a board member in several privately held companies including his positions as Chairman of i-Zone Limited, Chairman of BlueCIoud Networks Limited, Chairman of Gudu Studios Limited and Vice Chairman of Achimota Golf Club where he previously served as Captain.

In the 2008 elections in Ghana, Mr. Amoafo-Yeboah contested for the high office of President as an Independent Candidate on the singular theme of job and opportunity creation for all Ghanaians.


Mr. Amoafo-Yeboah was subsequently appointed Chairman of the Ghana Venture Capital Trust Fund from 2013 to 2017.
desc: Mr. Kwesi Amoafo-Yeboah received a BS degree in Engineering and Petro-Physics from San Francisco State University, San Francisco, California - USA.

name: Mr. Muhammed Rudman
about:
Muhammed Rudman is the pioneer CEO of the Internet Exchange Point of Nigeria (IXPN), Nigeria's first and only neutral IXP. A seasoned ICT professional with over 20 years of extensive experience in network design, implementation, optimization, and management. Rudman worked in technical and management roles for numerous firms before joining IXPN, including Galaxy IT & T Limited.


He is a member of several professional organizations both within and outside of Nigeria and has made significant contributions to the development of the country's ICT sector.


He presently serves as Vice President of the Association of Telecommunications Companies of Nigeria (ATCON) and as Chairman of the Nigeria IPv6 Council, a branch of the global IPv6 Forum. Rudman is also a board member of the African Internet exchange Points Association.


Rudman served as the Executive President of the Nigerian Internet Registration Association (NiRA) from 2019 to 2023. NiRA is the administrator of the .ng ccTLD (country code Top Level Domain) on behalf of Nigeria.


He has served on a number of strategic and policy-making committees across the country. Rudman served on the Presidential Committee on the National Broadband Plan 2020-2025 and the Committee to Review National Cybersecurity Policy and Strategy 2021. He was recognized as one of the 100 Leading Telecom & ICT Personalities in Nigeria in 2018 by ATCON (Association of Telecommunications Companies of Nigeria).
desc: Muhammed Rudman is the pioneer CEO of the Internet Exchange Point of Nigeria (IXPN), Nigeria's first and only neutral IXP.

name: Dr. Babatunde Obrimah
desc: Dr. Babatunde Obrimah, a fintech expert, leads ELBI Integrated Resources, advising on finance and AgriTech. He serves on boards at ARM MFB and Voyse Technologies.
about:
Dr. Babatunde Oghenobruche Obrimah holds a Doctorate in Business Administration (D.B.A.), an M.B.A., and B.Sc./M.Sc. degrees in Agronomy. He is also certified in Project Management (PMP) and Life Coaching, with additional training in Artificial Intelligence, Creative Thinking, International Trade, Leadership, Marketing, Public-Private Partnerships (PPP), and Digital Transformation at MIT. He is a Fellow of the Nigeria Institute of Management (FNIM) and an Honorary Senior Member of the Chartered Institute of Bankers (HCIB).

Dr. Obrimah began his professional career at First Bank Nigeria Plc, where he held various roles, including Group Head for Consumer Banking, Agriculture/SMEs, Infrastructures, Food/Beverages, and Investment Banking, as well as Regional Director for Commercial Banking in the South-south and South-east regions. He also contributed to agricultural lending initiatives at the Nigeria Incentive-Based Risk Management System for Agricultural Lending (NIRSAL Plc). Later, he served as Managing Director/CEO of ELBI Integrated Resources Limited, a consulting firm providing advisory and project management services to banks and financial institutions, with a focus on agricultural financing. Until recently, he was the Chief Operating Officer of the Fintech Association of Nigeria. Currently, he is the Lead Consultant at ELBI Integrated Resources Limited, advising financial institutions, fintechs, and agricultural projects.

A passionate advocate for fintech innovation, Dr. Obrimah believes technology platforms are critical enablers of economic growth. He promotes technological advancements in verticals such as AgriTech, PropTech, TransTech, InsurTech, EdTech, and HealthTech, emphasizing good governance and ethical practices in the fintech ecosystem. He serves as an Independent Non-Executive Director on the boards of ARM MFB, Voyse Technologies (Chippercash), and IHMS Limited (an HMO). Additionally, he is on the advisory board of Vank Limited, a PropTech company leveraging tokenization for commercial property funding solutions.

Abiola is a lawyer, an IT enthusiast, and a governance professional with over 20 years of experience across multiple industries in Nigeria, with a stint in Dubai, UAE. She is a certified IFC Corporate Governance Trainer and a member of the UK Institute of Leadership and Management.


She is the CEO of Edniesal Consulting, a firm offering unique corporate services to clients across different sectors. She is also the Founder of The CIO Club Africa, the Convener of The CIO Awards Africa, and The Ladies-in-Tech Network—strategic platforms that foster collaboration among stakeholders to drive Africa's digital transformation agenda.


Abiola serves on several boards, including SIB Insurance Brokers and the Beer Sectoral Group of the Manufacturers Association of Nigeria (MAN). She is also the Chairman of NOKASH, a fintech company. Additionally, she is a member of the Governing Council of the Institute of Chartered Secretaries and Administrators of Nigeria (ICSAN) and serves as the Honorary Secretary of the Young Directors Forum of the Institute of Directors (IOD). She is also a member of the Chartered Institute of Arbitrators (UK) and a director of the Strap & Safe Child Foundation. Previously, she chaired the ICSAN Lagos State Chapter.


Abiola has held several executive leadership positions throughout her career. She was the Group General Counsel, Company Secretary, and Head of Corporate Services at PZ Cussons Africa, as well as the Executive Secretary of PZ Cussons Foundation. Prior to that, she was the Legal Counsel, West Africa, and Deputy Company Secretary of Cadbury Nigeria Plc and Cadbury Ghana, both subsidiaries of the Mondelēz Group. She also served as a Senior Trust Executive at UBA Trustees.
desc: Abiola is a lawyer, IT enthusiast, and CEO of Edniesal Consulting, driving digital transformation in Africa.
name: Abiola Laseinde
